findings.evidence: `rg` shows the declaration at `MultiquadraticSplitting.lean:50-55`; its statement is `algebraMap ℤ (𝓞 K) m ∈ Q ↔ (p : ℤ) ∣ m` under only `[Q.LiesOver (span {(p : ℤ)})]`, with no use of `d`, `r`, `legendreSym`, or multiquadratic hypotheses.
findings.fix: Move this lemma to an earlier generic number-field file already imported here, e.g. `TauCeti/NumberTheory/NumberField/SplitsCompletely.lean` near the rational-prime `LiesOver` material, and use it from `MultiquadraticSplitting`.
findings.issue: `mem_prime_over_iff_dvd` is not multiquadratic-specific: it applies to any prime `Q : Ideal (𝓞 K)` lying over `(p)` and only unfolds `Q.under ℤ = span {(p : ℤ)}`.

summary: One extracted helper is generic number-field/LiesOver infrastructure but is placed as a private multiquadratic-splitting lemma. The rest of the private decomposition is naturally local to this theorem, and the imports do not show an evident problem.
